evals.prompt: check for feature drift across all my BDD tests — unit, a11y, and e2e
evals.expected_output: A drift report showing all file pairs are matched with no orphaned files.
assertions: The skill scans src/**/*.feature and src/**/*.steps.tsx for unit tests
The skill scans src/**/*.a11y.feature and src/**/*.a11y.steps.tsx for a11y tests
The skill scans e2e/features/*.feature and e2e/steps/*.steps.ts for E2E tests
The skill excludes sharedSteps.ts and sharedA11ySteps.ts from orphan detection
A summary table is presented showing total files, matched pairs, and orphaned count
The report confirms zero orphaned files

evals.prompt: run drift detection — I think I deleted a component but left its test files behind
evals.expected_output: The drift report identifies orphaned test files where the source component no longer exists.
assertions: The skill checks that the source component (.tsx) exists for each test file pair
Orphaned files are flagged with status ORPHANED in the report
The report explains why each file is considered orphaned
Suggested fixes are presented (delete, create counterpart, or rename)
The skill does NOT delete any files automatically

evals.prompt: check drift for a11y tests only — I renamed some components and I'm not sure all the a11y test files got updated
evals.expected_output: A drift report scoped to a11y tests showing any .a11y.feature files without matching .a11y.steps.tsx and vice versa.
assertions: The skill only scans a11y files (*.a11y.feature and *.a11y.steps.tsx), not unit or E2E
For each .a11y.feature, the skill checks for a matching .a11y.steps.tsx in the same directory
For each .a11y.steps.tsx, the skill checks for a matching .a11y.feature in the same directory
Mismatched files are reported with suggested renames
The skill does NOT modify any files without user confirmation
